sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
Radiation is used in five clinical scenarios: resectable/borderline resectable (neoadjuvant setting), resected (adjuvant), locally advanced, palliative, and recurrent disease [PANC-H 1 of 7]. Goals include sterilizing vessel margins, enhancing likelihood of margin-negative resection, providing adequate local control to prevent/delay progression, and palliating pain, bleeding, or obstructive symptoms [PANC-H 1 of 7]. Based on LAP-07 trial data, there is no clear survival benefit with addition of conventional chemoradiation following gemcitabine monotherapy; however, chemoradiation may improve local control and delay the need for resumption therapy [PANC-G 3 of 13 footnote i, reference Hammel P, et al. JAMA 2016;315:1844-1853].

Principles:
- Patients are best cared for by a multidisciplinary team [PANC-H 1 of 7].
- Prior to initiation of RT, staging is optimally determined with contrast-enhanced abdominal CT and/or MRI [PANC-H 1 of 7].
- IMRT is preferred over 3D-CRT for conventional or hypofractionated RT, particularly if dose escalation is considered [PANC-H 2 of 7].
- A motion management strategy should be considered, including 4D-CT, respiratory gating, breath-hold, respiratory tracking, or abdominal compression [PANC-H 2 of 7].
- The role and definition of elective target volume irradiation remain unclear, but emerging data suggest value to elective targeting of at-risk anatomic sites (Triangle Volume) and/or regional nodal basins. More prospective data needed [PANC-H 2 of 7, reference 6].
- Fiducial markers (1–5, preferably ≥3) placed under EUS may be useful for targeting [PANC-H 2 of 7].
- SBRT should be delivered at experienced, high-volume center with image-guided RT technology or in clinical trial [PANC-H 4 of 7 footnote c].
- SBRT should be avoided if direct invasion of bowel or stomach is observed [PANC-H 4 of 7 footnote d].

Dose Frameworks:
- Neoadjuvant/Definitive Chemoradiation: 36–56 Gy in 1.8–2.4 Gy fractions, 20–30 fractions, daily 5 days/week. Indication: Resectable/borderline resectable (neoadjuvant), locally advanced disease, recurrent disease.
- Adjuvant Chemoradiation: 45–50.4 Gy in 1.8–2.0 Gy fractions, 25–28 fractions, daily 5 days/week. Indication: Post-resection with positive margins or high-risk features.
- SBRT (3-fraction): 30–45 Gy in 10–15 Gy fractions, 3 fractions. Indication: Locally advanced, recurrent, or select borderline resectable disease.
- SBRT (5-fraction): 25–50 Gy in 5–10 Gy fractions, 5 fractions. Indication: Locally advanced, recurrent, or select borderline resectable disease.
- SBRT (hypofractionated, protracted): 67.5–75 Gy in 4.5–3 Gy fractions, 15–25 fractions. Indication: Locally advanced disease; caution with higher doses.
- Palliative RT: Variable dose/fractions, based on burden, tolerance, survival. Indication: Pain, bleeding, obstructive symptoms.
- Single Fraction SBRT (Celiac Plexus): Variable dose, single fraction. Indication: Celiac axis pain relief.

Approaches:
- Neoadjuvant Chemoradiation (Resectable/Borderline Resectable): Dose fractionation 36 Gy in 2.4 Gy fractions to 45–54 Gy in 1.8–2.0 Gy fractions (doses higher than 54 Gy may be considered in clinical trial). Concurrent chemotherapy: Capecitabine or Continuous infusion Fluorouracil (preferred); Gemcitabine (other recommended). Indication: Neoadjuvant setting. Key trials: PREOPANC, Alliance A021101. Toxicities: GI, hematologic; IMRT may reduce grade 3/4 GI toxicities.
- Adjuvant Chemoradiation: Dose fractionation 45–50.4 Gy in 1.8–2.0 Gy fractions (25–28 fx) to tumor b

timeline.title: Neoadjuvant/Definitive Chemoradiation
timeline.body: Total dose: 36–56 Gy, Fractions: 20–30, Dose per fraction: 1.8–2.4 Gy

timeline.title: Adjuvant Chemoradiation
timeline.body: Total dose: 45–50.4 Gy, Fractions: 25–28, Dose per fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy

timeline.title: SBRT (3-fraction)
timeline.body: Total dose: 30–45 Gy, Fractions: 3, Dose per fraction: 10–15 Gy

timeline.title: SBRT (5-fraction)
timeline.body: Total dose: 25–50 Gy, Fractions: 5, Dose per fraction: 5–10 Gy

timeline.title: SBRT (hypofractionated, protracted)
timeline.body: Total dose: 67.5–75 Gy, Fractions: 15–25, Dose per fraction: 4.5–3 Gy

timeline.title: Palliative RT
timeline.body: Total dose: Variable, Fractions: Variable, Dose per fraction: Variable

timeline.title: Single Fraction SBRT (Celiac Plexus)
timeline.body: Total dose: Variable, Fractions: 1, Dose per fraction: Single fraction
